title: KIA Captures Six SAC Frontline Positions Near Maw Wan Gyi, Hpakant Township
detail: The Kachin Independence Army (KIA) captured six Myanmar military council frontline positions near Maw Wan Gyi in Hpakant Township, Kachin State, following intense clashes that began on April 1. Hpakant is Myanmar's jade-mining heartland and a strategically important revenue source for the junta; sustained KIA pressure there threatens both SAC military footholds and jade extraction revenues. The captures continued the KIA's multi-month campaign to isolate the SAC garrison at Myitkyina, Kachin State capital, by degrading forward positions. Junta casualty figures were not immediately available.

title: KNLA Attacks SAC Railway Gate Outpost, Kills Five Soldiers — One Conscript Defects
detail: Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA) forces attacked a Myanmar military council railway gate outpost in the Ledo area, killing five SAC soldiers. One forcibly conscripted soldier surrendered and defected to the KNLA during or after the engagement — reflecting the continuing problem of low morale and forced conscription within SAC frontline units. The KNLA controls significant territory in Karen (Kayin) State and has intensified operations along infrastructure corridors since late 2023.
